name: KrillApp.Trigger.Color
title: Color Trigger
description: Triggers an alarm when the observed DataPoint's color value falls within a configured RGB range. Decomposes the color integer into R, G, B channels and checks each against min/max bounds. Fires when all three channels are within their respective ranges — nodes observing this trigger as a source are then invoked. Sets node state to WARN on match.
shortDescription: Triggers when a DataPoint color falls within a configured RGB range.
llmPurpose: Evaluates whether the observed DataPoint's color value falls within per-channel RGB min/max ranges; on match it raises an alarm state and fires, invoking its observers.
llmBehavior: invoked when the observed DataPoint updates (SOURCE_INVOKED)
reads the observed DataPoint's snapshot color value
compares each channel against min/max range
sets node state to WARN on match
on match, fires; nodes observing this trigger are invoked
llmOutputs: observers invoked when color is within range
llmCreationHints: Create a Color trigger when the user wants to detect a specific color range from a COLOR DataPoint. Configure per-channel RGB min/max bounds, and wire the DataPoint into the trigger's sources (with SOURCE_INVOKED) and inputs. Useful for pH indicator monitoring, water quality sensors, and colorimetric test analysis.

llmConnectionHints.role: conditional trigger that fires (invoking its observers) when the observed DataPoint's color falls within the configured RGB range
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Observer-side: list the COLOR DataPoint in sources (SOURCE_INVOKED) and inputs; actions that should react list this trigger in their own sources.

llmExamples: Alert me when the pH indicator turns red (R > 180, G < 80, B < 60).
Trigger a webhook when the water color shifts to green.
Warn me when the test strip color matches the target range.
